title: Attempt to download file from url, if fails assume Dockerfile
body:
If you do a
podman bud http://example.com/Dockerfile

podman currently assumes the file is an archive, if it is not an archive
we should assume it is a Dockerfile, download and create a Dockerfile in the
directory.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
Tested a build here and it worked cleanly and '-f <url> .' behaviour is also maintained so LGTM 👍.

With regard to documentation, I find **[context]** in this initial command description fairly ambiguous.
https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/blob/master/docs/buildah-bud.md

With this change, the last parameter should be either a `PATH` or `URL` (previously was `PATH`), Do the square brackets denote optional by convention? I don't think bud will execute if `PATH` or `URL` aren't provided, so this is actually a mandatory option?

Alternate wording option for **Description**

> The build context directory can be specified as the http(s) URL of an archive or Dockerfile. The contents of the URL is downloaded to a temporary location and, if required, extracted before execution.

It would be nice to add what type of archive it supports. Is it only the archive of a Dockerfile directory, or is it an archive of a container image? Is there a standard convention for the name for this type of archive? 'Dockerfile archive' ?
